cases.title: Testing-plan §3 Attempt/audit coverage row requires 100% attendance mutation audit completeness
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 Attempt/audit coverage block applies (FR-22, FR-29, FR-30, BR-22, BR-23, AC-18, AC-19).
dispute-trail fixture provides lecturer correction, admin override, and close-time absent assignments.
M08 Audit and Compliance module persists audit_logs with real database.
steps: Enumerate all committed AttendanceRecord create/update/delete mutations in the fixture (manual correction, admin override, bulk correction, and system close assignments).
Query audit_logs for each mutation by targetType AttendanceRecord, targetId, and occurredAt window.
Verify each audit row includes actorUserId, timestamp, target student and session, oldValue/oldStatus, newValue/newStatus, reason where mandated, and correlationId.
Compare mutation count to audit row count for the same correlation scope.
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix maps to testing-plan §3.1 Attempt/audit coverage: every committed attendance mutation has exactly one queryable AuditLog record with full CMP-01 fields per BR-22 and FR-29; zero mutation-audit gaps.

cases.title: M08 writeAuditEvent persists lecturer roster edit audit row atomically with M05 ledger update
preconditions: Real database transaction spans M05 Attendance Ledger and M08 Audit and Compliance modules.
Closed session exists with enrolled student AttendanceRecord status Absent.
Assigned Lecturer is within manualEditWindow per M06 policy resolution.
Policy mandates reason for manual corrections.
steps: Capture beforeState: AttendanceRecord status Absent; count audit_logs rows for target studentUserId and classSessionId.
Invoke M05 correctAttendance through application service with status Manual Present, reason, actor Lecturer, and Idempotency-Key.
Query attendance_records and audit_logs tables within the same test transaction scope.
Verify M08 writeAuditEvent payload includes actorUserId, timestamp, target student/session, oldStatus Absent, newStatus Manual Present, reason, and correlationId.
Assert audit_logs row is append-only and immutable after commit.
cases.expected: M08 module boundary writes exactly one audit_logs row atomically with attendance mutation: actor ID, timestamp, target student and session, previous status Absent, new status Manual Present, reason, and correlation ID per BR-22 100% coverage requirement.

cases.title: M08 audit service records system-actor rows for each close-time auto-absent assignment
preconditions: Open session exists with at least three enrolled students in Pending state without successful check-in.
M03 session close processor triggers M05 absent finalization as system process.
Real database with audit_logs append-only constraints.
steps: Execute POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close through session lifecycle service.
Query attendance_records confirming Pending students transitioned to Absent.
Query audit_logs filtered by actionType attendance_status_mutation and classSessionId for close-time events.
Verify each auto-absent student has a distinct audit row with system actor context per BR-22 exception, oldStatus Pending, newStatus Absent, timestamp, and correlationId.
cases.expected: 100% coverage for system-process attendance mutations: each close-time Absent assignment writes one audit_logs row logged as system actor per BR-22 exception with actor context, timestamp, target student/session, previous Pending, new Absent.

cases.title: M08 audit service persists admin override audit row with full before/after status fields
preconditions: Closed session exists with student AttendanceRecord status Absent after lecturer edit window expired.
AcademicAdmin actor is available with override authority per BR-16.
Policy requires documented reason for admin corrections.
steps: Capture beforeState AttendanceRecord status Absent and baseline audit_logs count.
Invoke admin correction service with status Excused, documented reason, and Idempotency-Key as AcademicAdmin.
Query attendance_records and audit_logs within the same DB transaction scope.
Verify audit row includes actorUserId AcademicAdmin, timestamp, target student/session, oldStatus Absent, newStatus Excused, reason, and correlationId.
cases.expected: Admin override mutation commits exactly one AuditLog row with actor ID, timestamp, target student and session, previous status, new status, reason, and correlation ID per BR-22 and BR-16.

cases.title: SM-03 attendance status transitions via manual and admin correction each produce auditable beforeState and afterState
preconditions: Integration database seeded with students in Absent, Present, and Late states on a Closed session.
Assigned Lecturer and AcademicAdmin actors are available within policy windows.
Audit query module can filter by targetId and actionType.
steps: Lecturer corrects Absent student to Manual Present with reason; capture beforeState, afterState, and audit row.
AcademicAdmin overrides Late student to Excused with reason; capture audit row.
Query audit_logs for both mutations and verify oldStatus/newStatus pairs match captured states.
Confirm checkInMethod Manual or Admin Correction is reflected where applicable.
cases.expected: Each legal SM-03 AttendanceRecord transition via roster edit or admin override commits with a queryable audit record documenting beforeState, afterState, actor, reason, timestamp, and correlationId per BR-22.

cases.title: Idempotent attendance mutation replay returns prior summary without duplicate audit_logs rows
preconditions: Closed session with Absent student exists; assigned Lecturer is within edit window.
Idempotency-Key support is enabled for PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId}.
Real database with audit_logs immutability constraints.
steps: Issue PATCH correction with status Manual Present, reason, and Idempotency-Key K1; record audit row id and count.
Replay identical PATCH with same Idempotency-Key K1, same body, and same actor.
Count audit_logs rows for the student-session pair and compare second response to first.
Verify AttendanceRecord afterState unchanged from first successful mutation.
cases.expected: First committed mutation writes exactly one audit row per BR-22; idempotent replay returns already-applied summary without a second audit_logs entry, preserving 100% coverage without duplicate audit records.

cases.title: PATCH attendance correction returns HTTP 200 and mutation is queryable via GET /v1/audit-logs
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated with bearer token.
Closed session exists with student AttendanceRecord status Absent; manual edit window is active.
AcademicAdmin bearer token can query audit logs per role policy.
steps: Lecturer calls PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Manual Present, reason, and Idempotency-Key; confirm HTTP 200.
AcademicAdmin calls GET /v1/audit-logs?targetType=AttendanceRecord&targetId={studentUserId}&actionType=manual_update&from={mutationStart}&to={mutationEnd}&page=1&pageSize=25.
Inspect HTTP 200 response envelope, meta.pagination, and matching audit entry payload fields.
Verify error is null and data array contains the committed mutation audit row with actorUserId, oldStatus, newStatus, reason, occurredAt, and correlationId.
cases.expected: PATCH returns HTTP 200 and committed mutation is queryable via GET /v1/audit-logs with standard envelope; audit entry includes all BR-22 required fields per API §4.7 and CMP-01.

cases.title: POST /v1/exports/attendance returns HTTP 202 and writes export audit entry per BR-22 and DI-09
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated with bearer token for section A.
Attendance records exist for section A within export scope.
Audit log baseline count for export actions is captured.
steps: Lecturer calls POST /v1/exports/attendance with format csv, scoped filters for assigned section A, and Idempotency-Key.
Confirm HTTP 202 with exportJobId and status Queued in standard envelope.
Wait for or poll export completion and query GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=export&actorUserId={lecturerId}&from={exportStart}&to={exportEnd}.
Verify audit row includes actor, timestamp, scope filter, format csv, and correlationId per CMP-02.
cases.expected: Successful export emits exactly one AuditLog entry with actor, timestamp, scope, and format per BR-22, BR-18, FR-30, and DI-09; HTTP 202 envelope matches API §4.6.

cases.title: E2E-06 primary workflow lecturer manual correction produces complete BR-22 audit trail
preconditions: Lecturer opens and closes session for assigned section.
Session close assigns Absent to enrolled student without successful check-in.
Manual edit window from effective policy is active.
Authorized role can query GET /v1/audit-logs.
steps: Lecturer reviews GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance showing student Absent.
Lecturer submits PATCH correction to Manual Present with documented reason.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs filtered by classSessionId and studentUserId for manual_update action.
Verify audit fields include actor ID, timestamp, target student and session, previous status Absent, new status Manual Present, reason, and correlation ID.
cases.expected: End-to-end E2E-06 path writes queryable audit record with 100% BR-22 field coverage: actor, timestamp, target student/session, previous status, new status, reason, and correlation ID per CMP-01.

cases.title: Flow C audit verification after AcademicAdmin escalated attendance override mutation
preconditions: Closed session exists with student AttendanceRecord status Absent after lecturer edit window expired.
AcademicAdmin is authenticated with override authority per BR-16.
SystemAuditor read scope can query audit logs.
steps: Record roster beforeState via GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance.
AcademicAdmin applies PATCH correction with status Excused and documented override reason.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs with filters targetType AttendanceRecord, targetId, actionType admin_override, and time range.
Verify audit payload includes actor AcademicAdmin, old status, new status Excused, reason, correlationId, and occurredAt.
cases.expected: Committed admin override mutation has corresponding queryable audit record with full BR-22 fields; no mutation-audit gap per AC-19.

cases.title: Alternate branch session close finalization writes system-actor audit row per auto-absent mutation
preconditions: Open session has at least five enrolled students with mixed Present and Pending states.
Lecturer bearer token is available for session close.
AcademicAdmin can query audit logs institution-scoped.
steps: Lecturer calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close and records closedAt.
Query GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance confirming Pending students became Absent.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs?targetType=AttendanceRecord&classSessionId={sessionId}&actionType=status_finalization&from={closedAt}&page=1&pageSize=100.
Count audit entries against number of students whose status mutated at close and verify each includes system actor, oldStatus Pending, newStatus Absent, and correlationId.
cases.expected: Each system-process absent assignment at close has a queryable audit record logged as system actor per BR-22 exception; audit count matches committed mutation count with full field set.

cases.title: GET /v1/audit-logs pagination returns scoped attendance mutation entries across page boundaries
preconditions: dispute-trail fixture includes at least 30 committed attendance status mutation audit entries for one class section.
AcademicAdmin bearer token is authenticated within institution scope.
Mutations span lecturer corrections and close-time absent assignments with full CMP-01 fields.
steps: Query GET /v1/audit-logs?targetType=AttendanceRecord&classSectionId={sectionId}&from={rangeStart}&to={rangeEnd}&page=1&pageSize=25.
Verify HTTP 200, meta.pagination page=1, pageSize=25, totalItems >= 30, and totalPages >= 2.
Record audit entry IDs from page 1 and verify each row includes actor, oldStatus, newStatus, reason where required, and occurredAt.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs with same filters and page=2; verify distinct IDs with no overlap.
cases.expected: Paginated audit query honors page and pageSize invariants; all BR-22 mutation audit entries remain queryable across pages with complete actor, timestamp, target, previous value, new value, reason, and correlationId fields.

cases.title: Student and cross-scope Lecturer denied audit log read without leaking mutation details
preconditions: Committed attendance mutation audit exists for session in section A with known target student.
Student bearer token and Lecturer token for unassigned section B are available.
AcademicAdmin token is available as authorized control for audit query.
steps: Student calls GET /v1/audit-logs?targetId={studentUserId}&classSessionId={sessionIdA}.
Out-of-scope Lecturer (section B) calls GET /v1/audit-logs filtered to section A mutation events.
Inspect responses for partial leakage of oldStatus, newStatus, actor, reason, or correlationId fields.
AcademicAdmin calls the same audit query as control and confirms HTTP 200 with full audit payload.
cases.expected: Student receives HTTP 403 Forbidden per AuditLog.read denial; cross-scope Lecturer receives HTTP 403 OutOfScope without partial mutation data; AcademicAdmin control returns HTTP 200 with full BR-22 audit fields per BR-19.

cases.title: Rejected PATCH correction outside edit window writes no attendance mutation audit row
preconditions: Closed session with Absent student exists; manual edit window has expired for Lecturer.
Lecturer bearer token is authenticated for assigned section.
Audit log baseline count for the student-session pair is captured before test.
steps: Call PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Manual Present and reason as Lecturer.
Confirm HTTP 409 EditWindowExpired; AttendanceRecord remains Absent.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs and audit_logs table for new manual_update rows tied to the rejected request.
Verify no committed mutation audit exists documenting old/new status change.
cases.expected: Rejected correction returns documented error without AttendanceRecord mutation; no audit_logs row documents a successful status change for the failed attempt; BR-22 applies only when mutation is committed.

cases.title: Student denied attendance export and no export audit row is created for unauthorized attempt
preconditions: Student bearer token is authenticated.
Attendance data exists for sections the student is not authorized to export institution-wide.
Export audit baseline count is captured.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/exports/attendance with format csv and broad filters.
Confirm HTTP 403 Forbidden with no partial export artifact.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs for export actionType tied to the student actor and request window.
Verify no successful export audit row was created for the denied request.
cases.expected: Student export denied per BR-18 and BR-19 without data leakage; no export AuditLog entry for unauthorized attempt; only successful exports produce audit rows per BR-22 and CMP-02.

cases.title: System Auditor PG-15 audit log review displays attendance mutation entry with old/new status columns
preconditions: Committed lecturer manual correction audit exists for closed session with known student.
SystemAuditor is authenticated on desktop browser with read-only audit scope.
PG-15 Audit log review route /audit/logs is available per FLOW-13.
steps: Navigate to /audit/logs and apply TableToolbar filters: targetType AttendanceRecord, actionType manual_update, and date range covering the mutation.
Locate AuditEntryRow for the committed correction in the paginated results table.
Verify row displays actor name, timestamp, target student/session identifiers, old status Absent, new status Manual Present, reason text, and correlation identifier.
Confirm pagination controls and default sort timestamp desc per PG-15 listing spec.
cases.expected: Browser FLOW-13 audit review journey displays queryable attendance mutation audit with all BR-22 required fields visible in AuditEntryRow per CMP-01; authorized SystemAuditor can investigate dispute trail without mutation privileges.
